documents.text: Hypertension is defined as systolic blood pressure ≥140 mmHg or diastolic blood pressure ≥90 mmHg on at least two occasions. First-line pharmacologic therapy includes ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs), thiazide diuretics, or calcium channel blockers. Lifestyle modifications—reduced sodium intake, weight loss, and physical activity—are recommended for all patients.

Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor indicated for hypertension and heart failure. Common adverse effects include cough, hyperkalemia, and angioedema. Monitor renal function and serum potassium, especially in patients with renal impairment. Contraindicated in pregnancy.

Type 2 diabetes mellitus is characterized by insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency. HbA1c ≥6.5% or fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL confirms the diagnosis. First-line pharmacotherapy is typically metformin, with GLP-1 agonists or SGLT2 inhibitors added for cardiovascular or renal benefit.

Blood pressure treatment targets: <130/80 mmHg for most adults with hypertension or elevated cardiovascular risk; <140/90 mmHg for lower-risk adults. Individualize targets based on comorbidities and tolerance.

Ramipril is an ACE inhibitor used for hypertension, heart failure, and post-MI management. Renal dose adjustment required for CrCl <50 mL/min. Avoid potassium supplements and potassium-sparing diuretics unless hypokalemia is present.

Lifestyle modifications for hypertension: DASH diet (high fruits, vegetables, low-fat dairy); sodium restriction to <2.3 g/day; weight loss if overweight; at least 150 min/week moderate exercise; limit alcohol to ≤1 drink/day for women, ≤2 for men.

In patients with diabetes and hypertension, target BP <130/80 mmHg. ACE inhibitors or ARBs are preferred for diabetic kidney disease. SGLT2 inhibitors and GLP-1 agonists reduce cardiovascular events in this population.

Enalapril is an ACE inhibitor for hypertension and heart failure. Onset of action 1 hour; peak effect 4–6 hours. Avoid in bilateral renal artery stenosis. May cause fetal injury; discontinue if pregnancy is detected.
